capacity_evidence.device: one Blackhole chip from a P300c endpoint (TT_VISIBLE_DEVICES=0)
capacity_evidence.bounded_strategy: QKV/O projections, full-attention SDPA, precise sparse experts, and retained dense controls use bounded 128-token work units with consumed intermediates explicitly deallocated.
capacity_evidence.advertised_context_result: PASS: real checkpoint layer 13 returned shape [1,1,131072,2880] with finite first and last logical tokens; 32.79 s test call.
capacity_evidence.next_failure: No device-capacity failure exists within the HF-advertised interval.
capacity_evidence.largest_all_metrics_pcc_result: Sliding/full prefill output PCC 0.99239488/0.99222492; following decode PCC 0.99686933/0.99658721; all reported K/V cache PCC values at least 0.99518482.
capacity_evidence.diagnostic_2048: Sliding passed all metrics. Full-attention output PCC passed at 0.991286, while value-cache PCC 0.989215 was 0.000785 below the strict 0.99 cache bar; the following decode was not run after that assertion.
capacity_evidence.qualification_scope: The S=131072 run is a real-weight device-capacity and finite-output qualification. Full HF-reference output/cache PCC uses S=1024 as the largest all-metrics point because an O(S^2) CPU oracle is not constructed at the advertised endpoint.

notes: The optimized decoder preserves arbitrary positive logical prefill lengths by internal padding/chunking and permanently tests non-aligned S=3, 17, 33, and the post-window S=129 boundary. Real sliding/full S=128 decode PCC is 0.996978/0.999349; S=129 is 0.996255/0.999360. Decode positions below 128 use TTNN SDPA decode. Positions at and above 128 use bounded sink-aware FP32 attention with HiFi2 QKV/O projections to prevent top-4 router changes at sensitive non-aligned positions. Persistent K/V uses BFP8_B; current-token K/V remains BF16 before cache writes.

fused_decoder_stage.capacity_change: The fused stage advanced real exercised prefill from S=17 to S=33 without changing the HF-advertised public capability.

optimized_decoder_stage.validated_decode_positions: 3 through 18, aligned 128, non-aligned 129, and 1024 after matching real-weight prefill
optimized_decoder_stage.cache_storage_change: BFP8_B replaces BF16 for persistent K/V. Callers select max_cache_len up to the public context; the emitted default remains 128 for compatibility.
optimized_decoder_stage.capacity_change: The earlier S=128 reduction was based on an avoidable unchunked dense implementation. After bounding live tensors, a progressive real-weight full-layer sweep passes the HF endpoint, so current_supported_context is restored to 131072.

classification.attention_op: scaled_dot_product_attention_decode with bounded sink-aware boundary path

emit_provenance.collective_collapse: Full Hugging Face weights replace emitted shards; attention and expert all-reduces plus routing mesh partition are absent from runtime.

dtype_policy.functional_baseline: BF16 weights, activations, and cache; TILE layout; DRAM by default
dtype_policy.exception: The emitted router promotes its activation and linear result to FP32 before BF16 top-k; decode heads use one workload-derived L1 height shard.
dtype_policy.optimized_policy: Hot decode uses BF16 activations/residuals/norms/router/attention weights, BFP8_B routed expert weights with LoFi sparse matmuls, and BFP8_B persistent K/V. Boundary decode projections use HiFi2. Sliding prefill uses BF16/HiFi2 sparse expert outputs; full prefill above S=128 uses bounded BF16/HiFi4 sparse chunks. Full-attention S=128 retains exact FP32 manual attention and decoder-local BF16 dense experts.
dtype_policy.deferred: Complete full-model datatype-frontier selection remains a later datatype-sweep stage.
